summary: Clean soundboard recording of a legendary ’69 show – soaring Dark Star and The Eleven shine amid solid overall mix.
review: The GD‑69-02‑22 set captures one of the band’s peak psychedelic runs, highlighted by an epic Dark Star > Cryptical Envelopment segue, a blistering The Eleven, and tight acoustic‑to‑electric transitions like Mountains of the Moon into Dark Star.  The soundboard source is generally clear and balanced, though a few listeners note mild mids‑frequency muddiness typical of early ’69 tapes.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews praise the performance (top‑ten, must‑hear) while most note a good‑to‑very‑good soundboard mix; only occasional comments about muffling keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced SBD; several reviewers mention crisp guitars and good separation, while a few note slight mids muddiness or minor distortion during Dark Star.

standout_songs: Mountains of the Moon > Dark Star
show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic opener flows into a seamless Mountains‑of‑the‑Moon → Dark Star transition, building to high‑energy second set; the band maintains momentum with tight segues and strong improvisational peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly described as “man possessed,” delivering fluid leads, powerful fuzz tones, and driving the band’s cosmic vibe.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s percussion highlighted on The Eleven finale; drum work noted as precise and energetic.
